title: PostgreSQL Full-Text Search, Ranking, Language, and Abuse Budgets · Go Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss Go decisions for postgresql full-text search, ranking, language, and abuse budgets and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, compiler, network, XML, PostgreSQL, race, load, accessibility, deployment, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for bounded explainable entry search. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, cau…
objectives: Build a provenance-aware tsvector from admitted title, summary, safe text content, author, and categories with weighting and language policy.
Convert bounded user input through an explicit plainto, websearch, or structured tsquery policy and reject pathological or empty requests.
Pair GIN and supporting indexes with representative EXPLAIN evidence, data scale, selectivity, write cost, and regression thresholds.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Feed Go Search Document · changed case · Quiz Feed Go Search Document
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go team handles case 8014 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove admitted full-text queries, language policy, GIN plans, deterministic ranking, and safe snippets; the release claim must bind source, migrations, dependencies, binary, image, configuration, and restored state; then change one tenant, cursor, reader-state, authorization, cache, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Build a provenance-aware tsvector from admitted title, summary, safe text content, author, and categories with weighting and language policy.

steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Feed Go Safe Snippets: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Go team handles case 3871 during a PostgreSQL claim and failover drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove admitted full-text queries, language policy, GIN plans, deterministic ranking, and safe snippets; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant operations must preserve the prior admitted state; then change one schema, constraint, transaction, upsert, migration, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Generate bounded snippets from safe text or sanitize marked fragments and expose matched terms without unsafe feed markup.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Generate bounded snippets from safe text or sanitize marked fragments and expose matched terms without unsafe feed markup.
